questions.text: Oxidation is defined as the _____ of electrons.
In a redox reaction, the reducing agent:
questions.explanation: The reducing agent reduces others by giving up its own electrons (it is oxidized).

questions.text: The oxidation number of Oxygen in most compounds is:
What is the oxidation number of Sulfur in H₂SO₄?
The oxidation number of an element in its free (uncombined) state is:
questions.explanation: Examples: Na, H₂, O₂, Cl₂ all have oxidation number 0.

questions.text: In the reaction Zn + Cu²⁺ → Zn²⁺ + Cu, which species is oxidized?
questions.explanation: Zn loses two electrons to become Zn²⁺.

questions.text: Oxidation number of Manganese in KMnO₄ is:
A disproportionation reaction is one in which:
options: The same element is both oxidized and reduced
questions.explanation: Example: 2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂ (Oxygen goes from -1 to -2 and 0).

questions.text: Which of the following is a strong oxidizing agent?
questions.explanation: Fluorine has the highest tendency to gain electrons.

questions.text: In a galvanic cell, oxidation occurs at the:
questions.explanation: Anode is the electrode where loss of electrons happens.

questions.text: Reduction is defined as the _____ of electrons.
Oxidation number of Hydrogen in metal hydrides (like LiH) is:
questions.explanation: Metals are more electropositive than Hydrogen.

questions.text: Standard reduction potential of Hydrogen electrode is:
Oxidation number of Cr in K₂Cr₂O₇ is:
Which method is used to balance redox reactions?
Oxidation number of Cl in ClO₄⁻ is:
The salt bridge in a cell maintains:
questions.explanation: By allowing ions to flow between half-cells.

questions.text: Which is not a redox reaction?
questions.explanation: Acid-base neutralization involves H⁺ transfer, not electron transfer.

questions.text: Electrochemical series lists elements in order of their:
Oxidation number of Fluorine is always:
In CH₄, oxidation number of Carbon is:
An oxidizing agent gets _____ in a reaction.
Standard Hydrogen Electrode (SHE) uses _____ catalyst.
Which metal can displace Copper from CuSO₄?
questions.explanation: Zinc is above Copper in the electrochemical series.

questions.text: Sum of oxidation numbers in a neutral molecule is:
Oxidation number of Carbon in CO₂ is:
In Daniel cell, Zn electrode is:
Reduction potential of Li is highly negative, making it a powerful:
questions.explanation: It has a very high tendency to lose electrons.

questions.text: The reaction Fe + Sn²⁺ → Fe²⁺ + Sn is spontaneous because:
options: Fe is more reactive than Sn
Sn is more reactive than Fe
questions.explanation: Fe has lower reduction potential than Sn.

questions.text: Oxidation number of Oxygen in OF₂ is:
questions.explanation: Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen.

questions.text: Combination of half-reactions gives a _____ reaction.
questions.explanation: Sum of oxidation and reduction halves.

questions.text: A negative E° value implies the electrode is a better _____ than Hydrogen.
questions.explanation: It loses electrons more easily than H₂.

questions.text: Oxidation number of P in PO₄³⁻ is:
Stock notation uses _____ to represent oxidation states.
In acidic medium, KMnO₄ is reduced to:
Balancing charge in ion-electron method is done by adding:
questions.explanation: Added to the side with more positive charge.

questions.text: Cathode is _____ charged in an electrolytic cell.
Oxidation number of N in NH₄⁺ is:
Rusting of iron is a _____ process.
questions.explanation: Iron is oxidized by oxygen in presence of moisture.

questions.text: Redox reactions are used in:
questions.explanation: Redox is fundamental to many fields.
